codeReview.summary: The feature delivers the requested responsive behavior: Leads and Storm Watch switch to mobile card layouts on small viewports, and the sidebar collapses behind a hamburger-triggered overlay that closes on outside click and nav selection. One non-blocking implementation issue was found in the Leads mobile card markup.

issues.description: The mobile row is rendered as a <button> that contains another <button> for the score badge trigger (inner button starts at line 243). Nested button elements are invalid HTML and can cause inconsistent focus/click behavior across browsers and assistive technologies. Replace one of the controls (e.g., outer wrapper as non-button with role/button handlers, or make score trigger non-button) to avoid interactive nesting.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker started/stopped the dev server with ad-hoc shell commands instead of the declared .factory/services.yaml web service commands, including a broad process-kill pattern that may terminate unrelated local dev sessions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows `npm run dev 2>&1 &` and `ps aux | grep "npm run dev" | grep -v grep | awk '{print $2}' | xargs kill`. .factory/services.yaml already defines `web.start`/`web.stop` for port 5173.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The react-frontend skill requires console-error checks after each UI interaction, but the procedure does not provide a concrete, auditable command sequence and the transcript has no explicit console-read actions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill file says: 'Check for console errors during all interactions'. Transcript skeleton for this feature shows viewport/open/click/screenshot actions only, so console verification is not directly evidenced.

summary: Reviewed commit 07fc6fe and transcript for worker session aebd2c65-459d-48d2-8a25-17ba6b3ae6e7. Feature requirements are met for mobile cards and sidebar overlay behavior; review passes with one non-blocking HTML/accessibility issue in LeadsTable mobile card markup.
